topic: Agno Framework Guide 2026: The Fastest Python AI Agent Library (Formerly Phidata)

key_points: Comprehensive overview of Agno's architecture: Agent, Team, Workflow layers
Covers fast instantiation (~3 microseconds) and low memory footprint (~5KB)
Explains model-agnostic design with plug-and-play LLM support
Production-ready features: memory, knowledge, MCP tool support
Comparison with LangGraph and CrewAI benchmarks
Step-by-step tutorial for building AI agents with Agno
Code examples for single agent and multi-agent setups
Step-by-step guide for beginners building their first Agno agent
Building a deep research agent using Agno

stats.fact: Agno achieves agent instantiation in approximately 3 microseconds — 5,000x faster than LangGraph
Agno's average memory footprint per agent is ~5KB — 50x more memory efficient than LangGraph
Agno has 39,100+ GitHub stars as of March 2026 and is one of the fastest-growing AI agent frameworks
Agno supports 100+ pre-built tool integrations including web search, data analysis, file operations, and APIs
Agno was rebranded from Phidata in January 2025; the name means 'pure' in Greek (ἁγνὸ)
Latest release v2.5.13 (March 2026) added ReliabilityEval for agent evaluation and enhanced AgentOS APIs
You can build a production-ready agent with memory and tools in ~20 lines of Python code using Agno
Agno natively supports text, image, audio, and video inputs — true multimodal from the ground up

angles: Why Agno's 5,000x speed advantage over LangGraph actually matters in production (concurrent sessions, cost per agent)
The Phidata-to-Agno rebrand story: from data engineering tooling to agentic OS, and what changed
Three-layer architecture (SDK → AgentOS → Control Plane UI) explained with real workflow examples
Agno vs LangGraph vs CrewAI vs AutoGen: which framework fits which use case in 2026
Building your first multi-agent pipeline in <50 lines: research → summarize → publish
Model-agnostic design: how to plug in OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, or local Ollama models
MCP (Model Context Protocol) support in Agno: connecting Slack, Drive, wikis to your agents
Production deployment checklist with AgentOS: sessions, memory, traces, and monitoring
outline: H2: What Is Agno? The Phidata Rebrand Explained
H2: Why Agno Is Claimed to Be the Fastest Python Agent Framework
H2: Core Architecture: Agent, Team, and Workflow
H3: Knowledge Base and Agentic RAG
H3: 100+ Tool Integrations and MCP Support
H3: Multimodal Inputs (Text, Image, Audio, Video)
H2: Getting Started: Install and First Agent in 5 Minutes
H2: Building a Multi-Agent Workflow: Step-by-Step Example
H2: Deploying to Production with AgentOS
H2: Agno vs LangGraph vs CrewAI vs AutoGen: Which Should You Use?
H2: Limitations and When NOT to Use Agno
H2: Conclusion: Is Agno the Right Framework for Your Python AI Projects in 2026?
